# Phylo view

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="49%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
library(ape)
library(ggtree)
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(42)
tree <- rtree(80)

# Pairwise tip distances; tips whose min distance to any other tip is small
d         <- cophenetic(tree)
min_dist  <- apply(d, 1, function(x) min(x[x > 0]))
threshold <- quantile(min_dist, 0.10)

tip_data <- data.frame(
  label = names(min_dist),
  group = ifelse(min_dist <= threshold, "close tip pairs", "other tips/nodes"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

ggtree(tree, color = "grey50", size = 0.5) %<+% tip_data +
  geom_nodepoint(aes(color = "other tips/nodes"), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_tippoint(aes(color = group), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = NULL,
    values = c("close tip pairs" = "#f75323", "other tips/nodes" = "grey50"),
    guide  = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4))
  ) +
  theme_tree() +
  theme(
    plot.background   = element_rect(fill = "#101f27", color = NA),
    panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "#101f27", color = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "#101f27", color = NA),
    legend.key        = element_rect(fill = "#101f27", color = NA),
    legend.text       = element_text(color = "white", size = 10),
    legend.position   = "bottom"
  )
```

:::
::: {.column width="49%"}
::: {style="font-size:1rem;"}

::: {.fragment}
- Let $x_{ij}$ ``mark'' evidence for transmission from $i$ to $j$.  
:::

::: {.fragment}
- Marks can be: 

  - phylogenetic or genetic distance\
    [Poon et al. VE 2016](https://academic.oup.com/ve/article/2/2/vew031/2742100), 
  - genetic distance and time elapsed\
    [Blenkinsop et al. SMMR 2025](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/09622802241309750), 
  - deep-sequence linkage and direction scores\
    [Wymant et al. MBE 2018](https://academic.oup.com/mbe/article/35/3/719/4653772?guestAccessKey=), 
  - using serohistory\
    [Ratmann et al. STM 2016](https://www.science.org/doi/abs/10.1126/scitranslmed.aad1863), 
  - using incidence assay\
    [Fisher et al. AIDS 2010](https://journals.lww.com/aidsonline/abstract/2010/07170/determinants_of_hiv_1_transmission_in_men_who_have.15.aspx), 
  - ...
:::

::: {.fragment}
- Consider high confidence pairs above $\eta > 0$ 
<div style="font-size:1.5rem;">
$$
 \mathbb{1}\{ x_{ij} > \eta \}
$$
</div>

- Count
<div style="font-size:1.5rem;">
$$
n_{ab} = \sum_{ij} \mathbb{1}\{ x_{ij} > \eta \} \mathbb{1}\{i \in a\} \mathbb{1}\{ j \in b \}
$$
</div>
:::

:::
:::
:::
